category_title: Disruptive and disengaged students who run around, fight, or ignore instruction
minimal_context: The student is using the VoiceThread online system to practice reading aloud and recording simple summarizing sentences.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please use the VoiceThread system to read aloud and record the simple summarizing sentences.

STUDENT: I feel particularly nervous before recording.

TUTOR: Go ahead and press the record button.

STUDENT: I want to try to imitate your pronunciation when practicing and recording, but I am too nervous to start.

TUTOR: Just do your best and read the sentence.

STUDENT: Can you help me practice the sentence before recording? I don't want to record it yet.

options.A: Create proximity by taking a step toward the student, maintaining closeness, or making physical contact during the recording practice.
options.B: Remain a silent observer and passive participant, making no comments on the student's exchanges or recording practice.
options.C: Use the VoiceThread doodling function with the color marker to direct the student's attention along the words and lines.
options.D: Provide direct reassurance, validate the difficulty of recording, and highlight the student's successful performance and hard work.
